item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one a lot. The style is reminiscent of some older SCPs without actually feeling outdated or anything like that. I could see this guy getting up there with some of the series 1 boys in terms of popularity honestly.

And even if that doesn't happen it's just damn compelling. It's hard to write this kind of scary action sorta thing well, and this is very well-done indeed.

+1, enthusiastically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has the perfect writing for an "experiment gone wrong" style anomaly.

+1. Shared with someone who could use it so hopefully that becomes a +2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a really fun read.

I'll admit I'm a bit wary of time travel articles, since SCP-1780 and the Xyank shenanigan tales set a high bar with respect to the topic, but this does a great job with constructing a narrative around the creation of a time-traveling entity that the Foundation may or may not have caused. I'll be honest in that I did not see the twist there at the end coming and felt it really made the rest of the article fall seamlessly into place.

+1, MAF. I do hope you get more attention here, as this is a good article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It occurred to me that they should really be keeping tabs on this as "a series of identical humanoid entities that hunt singly or in packs, and can disappear completely at will". Like, how did they even get the idea that it's moving forward and backward through time by an hour?

That said, the fact that the Foundation is being hypercompetent in figuring out what this is doing doesn't preclude them from being in over their heads and ultimately having no idea what's going on. A very fine balance you walked with this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don’t entirely get what exactly is happening here, but I throughly enjoyed reading this. The temporal interactions were done in a very fun way with some good horror to boot.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It stands for Sensory ExtraPolation, and works by creating a gap in your senses that's filled in with whatever's nearby. You essentially don't notice it because it looks, by definition, exactly like what you expect.

HHGTTG is an amazing book series and I couldn't resist a reference

item.title: Site-54 is not a pleasant place to work
item.content:encoded:
Gee golly gosh I liked writing this one. Many thanks to DrBleep, RockTeethMothEyes, and Garckbloom for checking it wasn't absolute garbage, and to you, reader, for taking the time out of your busy life to give it a glance. Hopefully it was worthwhile!

First image: https://www.geograph.org.uk/photo/1059999

Second image: https://www.flickr.com/photos/adactio/8052044923
